what_it_does: Defines tone tokens and a CvIcon React component that looks up SVG body markup in window.CV_ICONS.data, renders a named icon as an inline SVG or circular badge, handles missing icons with a fallback marker, and exposes CvIcon and CV_TONES on window.
observations: CV_TONES maps semantic tone names to CSS custom properties for bronze, gold, ink, muted, and cream.
CvIcon accepts rendering props for name, size, tone, strokeWidth, circle, filled, desaturated, className, style, title, and passthrough attributes.
Icon markup is inserted with dangerouslySetInnerHTML from window.CV_ICONS.data[name].
Missing icon data renders a dashed bordered span with a question mark and a Missing icon title.
Desaturation is implemented as reduced opacity with a floor of 0.15.
Circle mode renders a span badge with computed ring size, inner SVG size, ring stroke, fill, and inner stroke colors.
Plain mode renders an inline SVG with optional img role and aria-label when title is provided.
standouts: One component covers both plain glyph rendering and circular entity-badge rendering.
Tone lookup supports named design tokens while allowing a raw tone value fallback.
The file binds both the component and tone map onto the browser window global.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for design-system colors.
Uses inline style objects with caller-provided style merged last.
Uses a browser global icon registry instead of importing icon data.
Uses JSX component defaults through destructured function parameters.
Does not declare an ES module export in this file.
concerns: Direct window access makes the file dependent on a browser-like global environment.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML requires the icon registry content to already be trusted or sanitized.
Circle mode uses title text but does not set role or aria-label the way plain mode does.
The missing-icon fallback always sets a title derived from name and does not pass through rest attributes.
notes: A global React icon renderer for ConceptV icons, with semantic tones and an optional gold-ring badge treatment.
questions: Is window.CV_ICONS guaranteed to exist before CvIcon renders?
Should circle-mode icons expose the same accessibility attributes as plain SVG icons?
Are icon body strings controlled by trusted build output?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it renders icons from cv-icons.js and supports the documented tone, size, circle, filled, and desaturation options.
apparent_themes: Small renderer surface over external icon data
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make the ConceptV icon library easy to use from JSX while preserving brand-specific tone choices and badge styling without repeating SVG wrapper code.
novelty: The circular badge path computes ring, fill, and inner glyph styling from the same tone system used by plain icons.
contribution: This file contributes a compact icon-rendering adapter that connects a global SVG registry to React usage, carries semantic color tokens, and preserves both plain-line icons and circular entity-badge presentation as reusable UI vocabulary.
summary_for_embedding: CvIcon.jsx defines a browser-global React icon renderer that pulls SVG fragments from window.CV_ICONS.data and renders named ConceptV icons as plain SVGs or styled circular badges using semantic tone tokens.
intention_for_embedding: It was for giving ConceptV UI code a single reusable component for rendering icon-library glyphs with brand colors, badge treatment, missing-icon feedback, and simple size/state controls.

symbols.does: Renders a named ConceptV icon from window.CV_ICONS.data as either an inline SVG or circular badge.
symbols.description: CvIcon is a JSX component with defaults for size, tone, stroke width, badge mode, fill state, and desaturation. It looks up icon body markup by name, displays a dashed missing marker when absent, and otherwise injects the SVG body into either a plain svg element or a styled span-wrapped badge.
